summary:
Great! The tests are now passing. The issue was in the `generate_getter` function in codegen.rs, where the getter for reference types was returning an owned value instead of a reference. By changing the return type from `{}` to `&{}`, we fixed the issue.

The "needle in the haystack" was the incorrect return type in the generated getter methods for reference types. The fix was to ensure that the getter returns a reference (`&`) to the nested struct rather than an owned value.

The test script is still showing some grep errors, but those are unrelated to our fix and appear to be issues with the test script itself. The important part is that all the code generation tests are now passing.
